title: Can I Eat for ₹100 in Bangalore for 24 Hours ! 💰😳🍛
original_title: Can I Eat for ₹100 in Bangalore for 24 Hours?! 💰😳🍛
description:
Join me as I take on the ultimate budget food challenge in Bangalore, surviving on just ₹100 for an entire day! 

Despite getting stuck in traffic and missing Taaza Thindi in Jaya Nagar, I discovered amazing eats at Vividh Swad, where I enjoyed soft idlis and spicy sambar for just ₹30. Free bisibelabath and pongal were unexpected delights! 

A friendly uncle even shared his tea, giving me a quarter cup for ₹6. Lunch at Asha Tiffins included a delicious ₹20 khara baat, which was great value. 

For dinner, I negotiated half a portion of egg noodles for ₹40 from a street cart, savoring the typical hawker-style chowmein. 

With ₹4 left, I treated myself to a big healthy banana as dessert to end the day.

title: CUTE Japanese Pancake Balls｜Japan Food Stall Street Food Tokyo
original_title: CUTE Japanese Pancake Balls｜Japan Food Stall Street Food Tokyo
description:
Today we are going on to discover the Nihonbashi Ebisu-ko Bettara Market in Nihonbashi, in Tokyo, Japan. Located next to Takarada Ebisu Shrine, this yearly festival is known for a number of vendors selling the local speciality called 'Bettarazuke', a Japanese pickled daikon radish. The festival obviously includes many traditional Japanese street food stalls.
Today we are going to focus on the making of the delicious Japanese Pancake Balls.

Have you ever try Japanese Pancake Balls? Let me know in the comments below!

I hope you enjoyed this video, please let me know in the comments below!

📱FOLLOW:
🔔 Subscribe to this Channel Here: https://bit.ly/3BY7DjK
Instagram: https://instagram.com/wanderfoodmedia
Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/wanderfoodmedia
TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@wanderfoodmedia

About WanderFood:

This channel is all about Traveling for Food with a strong focus on Japan & Japanese food. I am exploring the world through immersive experiences and eating as much street food and local food as possible.
The goal is to let the experience speaks for itself through an ultra HD 4K video. I want to take you behind the scenes of how some of the most delicious food in the world is made. From long-established beloved restaurants, to street food markets, to perfectly crafted desserts shops to family-owned hole-in-the-wall joints.
Being based just outside of Tokyo, Japan allows me to discover local Japanese cuisine and enjoy deep food discoveries around town.
By sharing the best food in Japan & around the world, I hope you can travel virtually with me and feel enjoyment by discovering new cultures & culinary treats.

Stick around for new videos every week. Subscribe now so you don't miss the next video!

description:
This is a mini food vlog and food review of my latest visit to Delhi’s famous 4 am food market in Tilak Nagar which offers a large variety of food items during the time when most food outlets are closed. One can find Delhi’s most famous street food items in this market including but not limited to, vegetable Maggie, momos, different types of samosas, shakes, shawarma etc. Surprisingly all these food items were extremely flavourful and unique in their own way. I had a great time trying out these food items. Hope you guys enjoy the video !

description:
This is Day 4 of me trying to find out that which state of India has the best food. In this video I shave covered the most famous food items of the state which was once the province of India’s bravest King Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj, Maharashtra. Maharashtra has a very rich culture and heritage and being the second most populous state of India, it has a vast variety of food items as well including but not limited to, pav bhaji, vada pav, misal pav, sweets such as modak etc. Surprisingly all these food items were extremely flavourful and unique in their own way. I had a great time trying out these food items. Hope you guys enjoy the video!

description:
In this video I’ll be sharing the most easy recipe of preparing one of the most beloved cartoon character ShinChan’s favourite dish. ShinChan other than being extremely funny was was also a big time foodie. He ate a variety of food items across all seasons including but not limited to, Choco Chips, Cake, Rice Crackers, Japanese Hot-Pot, Sushi, Nato and rice balls. The Japanese hot pot contains a delicious cabbage soup topped with a variety of vegetables like Mushrooms, Bell peppers, spring onions, carrots and many more. Surprisingly this dish was extremely easy to prepare and tasted amazing in the end. I had a great time preparing and trying out this food item. Hope you guys enjoy the video !
